work: English Wiktionary — Old South Arabian-language lemma pages
conversion: Current main-namespace members of Category:Old South Arabian lemmas, Category:Sabaean lemmas, Category:Minaean lemmas and Category:Qatabanian lemmas fetched through the MediaWiki API. Every specific-language section is retained; a duplicate legacy Old South Arabian entry is omitted when the same page, script and part of speech has a specific-language entry. Lexical headings, primary head=/tr= pairs and top-level English senses extracted by scripts/import-wiktionary-ancient.mjs; Musnad is deterministically transliterated when a legacy head template omits tr=. The Wiktionary-internal legacy code sem-srb is retained as sourceLanguageCode rather than exposed as a BCP-47 lang. Every entry retains its exact source revision. Community-edited coverage; not a comprehensive Old South Arabian dictionary.

entries.def: Suffixal third-person dual pronoun: their two, of the two of them; them both; Attached to nouns, marking dual possession: their two, of them both; Attached to verbs or verbal nouns, marking a dual object: them both

Suffixal second-person singular pronoun: your; you; Attached to nouns, marking second-person singular possession: your; Attached to prepositions, marking a second-person singular object: you, to you, for you, etc; Attached to verbs, marking a second-person singular object: you

Suffix-conjugation ending marking the first-person singular and second-person singular; First-person singular suffix-conjugation ending: I; Second-person masculine singular suffix-conjugation ending: you; Second-person feminine singular suffix-conjugation ending: you

Used with a following clause-introducing pronoun or particle to mark cause, reason, or purpose: because, since, so that, in that
